message:
test(fuzz): commit crash artifacts found during DAG and bipartite fuzzing

Commits the libfuzzer crash-artifact set found while exploring the
new fuzz_dag and fuzz_bipartite targets, living under fuzz/artifacts/.
These are short (47–74 byte) inputs that drove fnx_algorithms into a
crash or panic and are preserved as regression seeds so subsequent
fuzz runs immediately replay them.

15 artifacts ranging 47–74 bytes each (~12 KB total). They are named
by SHA1 of the input as libfuzzer does by default and can be fed back
to the target via:

    cargo fuzz run fuzz_dag fuzz/artifacts/<sha1>

Keeping them under version control means a future contributor running
`cargo fuzz` on a fresh worker reproduces the same crash set before
mutating new inputs, which is the standard libfuzzer regression
pattern. No code changes — the underlying panics still need to be
triaged and either fixed or legitimately documented as expected
panic shapes.
